response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with ~15 years of experience, but their background is fundamentally mismatched with this role. The position requires SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. Adjacent experience (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments, brief embedded work) is too thin to bridge the gap to a principal-level hardware performance architecture role.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years educational path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching)
Some systems-level C/C++ and embedded exposure (National Instruments, robotics work)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon design, or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, or accelerator performance optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU workload experience
